title: Microdon parviflorus
taxonomy: Microdon parviflorus is classified as a species of fly in the family Syrphidae, commonly known as flower flies. This species is part of the genus Microdon, which consists of various species of flies. Microdon parviflorus is a type of solitary wasp mimic, meaning it has evolved to resemble a wasp to deter predators. The classification of Microdon parviflorus is further broken down into the kingdom Animalia, phylum Arthropoda, class Insecta, order Diptera, and family Syrphidae. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of this species.

intro: Microdon parviflorus, commonly known as Small-flowered Bugloss or Small Bugloss, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Microdon parviflorus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Small-flowered Bugloss is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in tinctures and infusions, to leverage its health benefits.
